updateMessage: Adds experimental Argentina 2026 resident tax rules, grouped employee social contributions, and Argentina-specific investment wrappers.

incomeTax.name: Impuesto a las Ganancias
incomeTax.tooltip: Resident Argentine ordinary-income tax for tax year 2026 using the published Article 94 annual scale. The non-taxable minimum is modeled as a pre-bracket deduction, while the Article 30 special employee deduction plus deductible employee social-security contributions are encoded as an employment-only piecewise credit. Spouse, child-count, and pensioner-specific deduction paths remain in the gap report.

socialContributions.name: Aportes personales a la seguridad social
socialContributions.tooltip: Grouped employee contribution proxy for Argentine dependent workers: 11% SIPA pension + 3% obra social + 3% INSSJP/PAMI, capped at the April 2026 monthly maximum contribution base annualized with a 13th-salary factor. Special regimes, month-by-month cap movements, and payroll-detail differences are documented in the gap report.

capitalGainsTax.name: Impuesto cedular
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: Simplified Argentine cedular capital-gains proxy using a 15% rate for taxable gains on non-exempt shares and similar financial assets. Public-offer listed local wrappers that the ruleset treats as exempt are modeled through zero-rate investment wrappers instead. Debt-instrument 5% cases, Article 100 special deductions, dividends, and detailed basket rules remain in the gap report.

helpText.label: Jubilación
helpText.contributionDescriptionP1:
Argentina does not expose a broad modeled tax-advantaged private pension lane in FinSim.

The ruleset therefore keeps the annual private-pension contribution cap at zero unless the engine is extended for narrower retirement-insurance deductions and product-specific rules.
helpText.contributionDescriptionP2:
Argentina does not expose a broad modeled tax-advantaged private pension lane in FinSim.

The same zero-cap limitation applies to both people in couple mode.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly ANSES retirement benefit.

FinSim uses a single state-pension age of 65 as a conservative proxy and does not model the real sex- and contribution-history-based eligibility split.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly ANSES retirement benefit.

FinSim uses the same simplified public-pension proxy for both people.

investmentTypes.label: CEDEARs
investmentTypes.helpText: Approximation of publicly offered CEDEARs traded on CNV-authorized Argentine markets. The ruleset treats sale gains as exempt. Cases that fail the public-offer exemption, dividend withholding detail, and product-specific edge cases remain outside the modeled scope.

investmentTypes.label: Merval
investmentTypes.helpText: Approximation of Argentine listed equities traded on authorized local markets and treated as exempt public-offer securities. Non-listed shares and transactions that fall outside the exemption are not modeled by this wrapper.

investmentTypes.label: Foreign Shares
investmentTypes.helpText: Approximation of non-exempt foreign listed shares held outside Argentina's exempt local public-offer wrappers. Gains use the modeled 15% cedular rate. Debt-instrument 5% cases, crypto-specific nuances, and exact foreign-broker reporting rules are documented in the gap report.

investmentTypes.label: RSUs
investmentTypes.helpText: Restricted stock awards are modeled as employment income when they vest or are received, then any later gain uses the modeled 15% cedular rate. Payroll withholding, exact basis mechanics, and cross-border sourcing details remain outside the current engine.
